You sit and converse with Etra for a while, letting your worries melt away. "Beautiful, isn't it?" he says, motioning to the gleeful crowd. Together you watch with smiles on your faces as the people of Spire dance, feast, and play.

Discard all  from Etra and add 1 mirth token to him. Each Ranger soothes 1 fatigue. Ready FUN AND GAMES.

213.12

If FRIENDS RECONCILED, go to 213.12a

You try to convince Bo to join the crowd, but he shakes his head. "That's okay. I'm having fun just watching. Honest." He lets out a wistful sigh. "Besides…the person I'd want to be with isn't here, anyway…" Despite his attitude, you get the feeling Bo is enjoying the festival in his own way. You spend some quiet time with him before rejoining the others.

Discard all  from Bo and add 1 mirth token to him. Each Ranger soothes 1 fatigue. Ready FUN AND GAMES.

213.12a

Together with Bo and Olyv, you sample every one of the confectioner's pastries until all of you are stuffed. "I can't eat another bite," Bo says, patting his belly.

"Wanna bet?" Olyv grabs one more roll and playfully jams it against Bo's lips. The two of them fall into a fit of laughter as he attempts to fight off the pastry attack.

Discard all  from Olyv and Bo and add 1 mirth token to each of them. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue. Ready FUN AND GAMES.

213.13

You tell Raemon that you'll help restock the stall in his place, and that he should go and enjoy himself. He protests, but eventually relents. Practically every time you see Raemon, he's working—it's nice to watch him unwind for once.

Discard all  from Raemon and add 1 mirth token to him. Each Ranger soothes 1 fatigue. Ready FUN AND GAMES.

213.14

You spend time together wordlessly enjoying the festivities. The music is lively and the food is delicious. Before long, you part ways, smiling and laughing, as you each set your sights on the next diversion.

Discard all  from this human and add 1 mirth token to them. Each Ranger soothes 1 fatigue. Ready FUN AND GAMES.

215.1

You hear a shriek as a swarm of bees descends upon them. If you don't act quickly, they're surely going to come to greater harm.

Add 1 to this human.

Clear this human with  to safely escort them away from the Cloudhive.

215.2

You hustle them away from the festival until you're certain they'll be safe from the cloudhive swarms. You tell them to stay put before running back into the fray.

Discard this human. Move any mirth on them to BUZZKILL.

215.3

You remove them from immediate danger, but it's too late—they've been stung all over. You take some time to perform first aid on them, first removing the many stingers, then applying a cooling medicinal salve from your first aid kit.

Discard this human. Each Ranger suffers 5 fatigue.

216.1

You've counted many beautiful days in the Valley, but today is shaping up to be particularly stunning. A soft breeze plays across your skin, and the sun rises over the mountaintops, casting its golden light upon the newly bloomed flowers that cover the hillside.

Hummingbirds and cloudhive bees flit here and there among them. You can't remember seeing so many flowers in one place in your entire life.

"A glorious morning, is it not?" You're stirred from your reverie by a man's voice calling from the trail. You recognize Raemon from your travels—he often runs caravans of both supplies and people to and from Spire. He approaches your camp and shakes your hand in greeting.

"It's always a pleasure to meet a Ranger on the trail. Looks like the weather is going to be perfect for the Blossom Festival, doesn't it? What good fortune!"

This is the first you've heard of a "Blossom Festival," but you're not surprised by the news. There's been abundant (and abnormal) floral growth all over the Valley for much of the current cycle, and Spire's Master of Revels, Etra Lash, is known to organize impromptu celebrations for occasions such as these. It is strange, you think to yourself, to see so many plants flowering at once—and so many out of season. You wonder aloud at the cause.

Raemon laughs. "I have responsibilites enough without troubling myself with such questions," he says. "But if you want to unravel the mysteries of nature, I wish you luck on your quest. May the ancestors guide you!"

With that, Raemon waves goodbye and returns to the trail. You see him join up with a caravan of hoversleds and stilt-horse wagons traveling in the direction of White Sky.

As you prepare for the day ahead, you imagine the delights that the Blossom Festival might hold—honeyed sunberry wine, the luxurious sounds of a dolewood lute, specially prepared foods and confections created by Spire's most talented chefs…

…perhaps you should go to the festival grounds of Brookside, the small village on the riverbank across from Spire itself, to see how preparations for the festival are coming along. Rangers are tasked with aiding the people of the Valley in whatever way they can, after all. Maybe they could use a hand.

You start down the trail, and watch as the cloudhive bees dance among the flowers.

On the day track, record Wild Growth underneath the weather for today, three days from now, six days from now, and nine days from now. On those days, use Wild Growth instead of the indicated weather.

Return the current weather to the collection and replace it with Wild Growth.

Record 216.2 ten days from now on the day track. If you completed REMOVE THE RECLAIMERS, record it six days from now instead. Gain the CAUSE FOR CELEBRATION mission.

Travel to Brookside to see how preparations for the Blossom Festival are coming along.

216.2

If you are on INVASION – STAGE II, SPIRE UNDER ATTACK!, or FALL OF SPIRE, record 216.2 tomorrow on the day track and do not read the rest of this entry. Otherwise, continue reading:

READ ONE:

If SPIRE FALLEN: You wake in a fret, your dreams haunted by terrifying visions. Your encounters with the reclaimers must have you on edge. But today is a new day—and an exciting one, at that. For despite the devastation caused by the reclaimer invasion, today is the day the Blossom Festival is set to begin in Brookside. You thought that perhaps the festival would be delayed on account of what happened in Spire, but the people are resilient and have already begun to rebuild. Perhaps a festival is just what they need to keep their spirits high.

Otherwise: You wake to the sound of birdsong and emerge from your tent, feeling refreshed. You smile as you remember that the Blossom Festival begins today in Brookside.

» Continue reading:

Complete each Festival mission.

Gain THE FESTIVITIES BEGIN mission.

Record 216.3 four days from now on the day track. Record Wild Growth underneath the weather for today and the next three days. On those days, use Wild Growth instead of the indicated weather. Return the current weather to the collection and replace it with Wild Growth.

Travel to Brookside soon to attend the Blossom Festival.

1.226

Arriving at the Terra Lodge, you spot a teenage boy shoveling snow just outside the entrance. His eyes widen when he sees you. "Oh! A Ranger!" he exclaims. "Are you here to see Mom? She's inside. I'll bring you to her." You trudge through the snow on your way to the entrance. This much snowfall is unusual for this time of year—no doubt caused by the storm that brought you here.

The boy leads you into the lodge's parlor, where several plush cushions are arranged around an enormous stone fireplace, flames crackling on the floor-level hearth.

Viv Terra sits atop one of the cushions, her piercing blue eyes scanning you from head to toe. "How serendipitous! It's a pleasure to see you. I was considering making the trek down to the Northern Outpost to talk to Lunn about this strange weather," she says, then motions toward the other seats by the fireplace. "Please, sit. Have some elderberry tea. Oz, would you fetch us a kettle?"

Oz nods and leaves the parlor, returning a few minutes later with a steaming kettle and a tea set on an elegantly carved wooden tray. You accept a warm cup, taking small sips by the fire as you and Viv exchange pleasantries. "I'm glad you're here. The snow and bitter cold blowing in from the north have me concerned for the local flora and wildlife." You nod in agreement. Such unseasonable weather is sure to disrupt the ecosystem, at the very least. You ask what you can do to help.

Viv stares into the fire for a moment before replying, her expression serious. "There's something unnatural at work. Can you not feel it? Even the spirits are disturbed."

You mention the dark clouds you observed over Sentinel Peak. "Yes, the storm has been there for some time now," Viv says. "Even as the rest of the clouds in the sky rush by, it's only grown in strength with the passing of each day. I fear my lodge and Lunn's outpost will soon be completely buried in snow if this continues."

You consider the problem. If the snowstorm isn't natural, what could be the cause? You decide that you need to get closer. Maybe you'll be able to discover the true nature of the storm from the top of Sentinel Peak. You share your thoughts with Viv.

She smiles warmly and pours you another cup of tea. "If you're going to reach the summit of Sentinel Peak, you'll need to be properly equipped. In fact—wait here a moment…" She puts the tea kettle down and heads into a nearby storeroom, returning moments later with a harness, a climbing hammer, and a set of metal pitons. "This climbing gear once belonged to my forefathers, the founders of Terra Lodge. They spent years scouring these mountains in search of the best location to construct this place. If you're heading up the mountain, you will be glad to have it."

You nod and accept the climbing gear, thanking Viv for her hospitality. "You're welcome here whenever you like. But before you go, a word of caution. The fingerprints of the ancestors are often present on otherwise unexplainable phenomena such as this. Be careful."

Gain the Climbing Harness reward card. Each Ranger may suffer 2 fatigue to swap a card in their hand for one copy of the Climbing Harness reward (this counts as swapping in a reward card as described on page 31 of the rulebook).

Gain the TO THE SUMMIT mission.

1.226a

You come at last to Sentinel Peak, the massive mountain at the northern edge of the Valley. Its slope is shadowed by the unwavering gray stormcloud at its summit. Standing beneath the cloud, you now feel certain that it is not a natural phenomenon. It hasn't moved an inch since you first spotted it, and has only grown in strength since then. Perhaps from the summit, you can investigate further and discern its true nature.

Gain the TO THE SUMMIT mission.

1.227

After a moment to rest and take in the spectacular view, you turn your attention to the ominous stormcloud that brought you here. In the center of the gray nimbus, you spot the silhouette of a rocky peak—another mountaintop, shrouded in the mist. But as you peer inside the storm, the astounding truth dawns on you. The rocky shape is not a mountain, but a floating island!

You stagger as the realization of what you are seeing takes hold. How could such a thing be possible? You shake your head, press your palms into your eyes, and look again. As before, the island continues to hang suspended in the clouds, a flurry of snow falling all around it. You stare in disbelief for several minutes before recovering your wits.

This is indeed no ordinary storm. Curiosity and a growing sense of adventure beckon you. Somehow, you have to find a way to get onto that island. But how? Peering about in thought, the solution presents itself to you: an old spiderline in disrepair, frost clinging to its nanowire. If you can fire a new spiderline from the nearby stanchion and latch it somewhere solid, you may be able to ride it to the island.

Complete the TO THE SUMMIT mission. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

Gain the ON THE LINE mission.

1.228

As you ride into the dense cloud, the details of the island become clearer. Its upper levels are home to a misty forest, along with the ruins of some ancient facility. Spires and antennas rise out of the structure. With a constant hiss, vents along the side of the rocky island belch out a steady stream of thick clouds—the very same clouds that masked the structure's presence. Below, the floating rock is riddled with cave entrances and metal walkways that weave throughout the caves, connecting smaller chunks of floating rock.

Your spiderline is latched to one of these precarious walkways along the underside of the island. The smell of ozone fills your nostrils as you ride further into the stormcloud. Approaching rapidly, the winds grow fiercer, but mercifully, your mag-grappler keeps its grip. If ever there was a time to be scared of heights, it would be now.

As soon as you reach a spot where you can dismount safely, you let go, landing on a circular metal deck with a resounding clank. All around you, the droning of machinery fills the air. Whatever this strange facility is, it appears to still be functional…but the storm around it threatens to throw the entire Valley out of balance. You rise to your feet and set forth, hoping that by exploring the island, you can figure out how to shut it down.

Complete the ON THE LINE mission. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

Gain the BEYOND THE MIST mission.

1.228a

With your preparations made, you aim the line launcher and fire. With a loud ka-chunk, the carbon nanotube rockets high into the sky. The recoil nearly sends you to the ground. Above, the wire springs forward like a striking snake, eventually disappearing into the clouds.

After a moment, the wire straightens, and a series of clicks emit from the launcher, indicating the line has taken hold of something solid on the other side.

At first, you worry about how steep the angle is, but to your surprise, the island appears to be bobbing up and down in a steady rhythm every few minutes. If you time it right, you should be able to attach yourself to this new line with a mag-grappler and ride it to the island. A shudder courses up your spine as you think about the consequences should you misjudge your timing. The last thing you want is to be stranded out on the line, thousands of feet above the ground.

Record FIRED A NEW LINE on the campaign tracker.

Ride the new spiderline from the summit of Sentinel Peak to reach the island within the stormcloud.

1.229

READ THE ENTRY MATCHING YOUR CURRENT LOCATION:


The Undergrove

Down here, in the island's underbelly, you find a variety of strange machinery: towering stone devices with metallic grating on all sides, dull glass globes that reveal nothing but a cloudy gray hue, and countless air shafts spewing out a constant stream of opalescent clouds.

Above you, through the haze, you spot what you can only assume to be the sophisticated technology keeping the facility aloft—metal half-spheres attached to the rocky exterior of the island, emitting a low hum as they allow the land mass to defy gravity itself. You recall reading that sonic tools were used during the Generational Projects to lift impossibly heavy objects. Perhaps what you are witnessing is that very same technology.

Given the state of this place, it's a wonder it's still floating at all. You ponder whether the facility was once staffed, or if it was set up to run autonomously. Nature has claimed, or perhaps reclaimed, many of the connected smaller islands and much of the interior caves. How long has this place been abandoned? How far has it remained hidden in the sky, looking like nothing but fluffy gray clouds from the ground below?

Check off the bubble matching your current location next to BEYOND THE MIST on your campaign tracker. If all three bubbles have been checked off, go to 1.229a.

You have studied the mist at this location. Travel to the locations not yet checked off to continue the mission.

Arbor Mists

Throughout the forest on the surface of the floating island, the mist is thick and heavy, an impenetrable fog that cloaks the landscape in gray. You can barely see more than a few meters in front of you at all times. But at the edge of your perception, you spot an ethereal figure moving in the fog—the only trace you've seen of anyone living here. When you call out, you get no response. Only a mere glance in your direction, and then the figure seems to dissolve once again into the fog.

You follow close behind, hoping to learn more. It's only then that you notice the way his gossamer form wavers through the mist. A spirit, perhaps? You wonder if they once maintained this entire facility alone. What a lonely existence that must have been.

If Nimbus Spirit is not in play, search the path deck and discard for him and put him into play. Do not read his entry.

Check off the bubble matching your current location next to BEYOND THE MIST on your campaign tracker. If all three bubbles have been checked off, go to 1.229a.

You have studied the mist at this location. Travel to the locations not yet checked off to continue the mission.

Nimbus Facility

Exploring the buildings on the far side of the island, you gather as much information about the facility as you can. Countless antennas and dishes litter the exterior of the facility. While some of the devices are broken beyond repair, most remain functional, despite what is likely centuries of wear and tear.

A central room, with tall windows overlooking the rest of the island, contains an elegantly crafted semi-circular surface of brushed metal. Rectangular panes of dusty glass are spaced evenly across the device, revealing nothing but your dim reflection. Only a single chair sits in the center of the chamber.

One wall of the room is devoted to a large diagram of the facility, surrounded by strange glyphs and illegible text. Even in this ancient illustration, dark gray clouds surround the structure, and snow emerges from the storm.

Check off the bubble matching your current location next to BEYOND THE MIST on your campaign tracker. If all three bubbles have been checked off, go to 1.229a.

You have studied the mist at this location. Travel to the locations not yet checked off to continue the mission.

1.229a

You take a moment to rest and put together what you know of this derelict facility. From what you understand, the structure must have been designed to influence the Earth's water cycle. It collected moisture in the air, purified pollutants, and generated rain clouds to transport and deposit water where it was most needed. You can only guess that flooding and drought were existential threats to the Earth during that time.

A sole keeper must have operated the station and lived here among its many instruments and weather-manipulating devices, until at long last he perished here, alone. Without his guidance, the station fell into disrepair, its machinery gone haywire. It probably drifted towards the Valley until it came to rest beside Sentinel Peak. It explains the stationary stormcloud, the blizzard, everything.

There has to be a way to shut the station down for good without causing the entire island to fall to the Earth. You can think of only one person who might know how—the keeper who once lived here. Perhaps it was his spirit you saw while wandering through the Arbor Mists outside the facility ruins. If you can commune with the spirit, you may be able to learn what to do next.

Complete the BEYOND THE MIST mission. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

Gain the GHOST OF A CHANCE mission.

1.230

You attune to your intuition, feeling the thoughts and emotions of the spirit flowing toward you like an isolated river in search of the sea. He knows why you’ve come, and is willing to help. You close your eyes and take a slow, measured breath.

Deep recognition shudders through you. Your consciousness expands. You sense feelings that are not your own, and see far off visions of life in distant lands. You see the island far to the north, hovering over unfamiliar mountains. Thunder rolls, and the tang of ozone bites your nostrils.

You see Nimbus Station as it once was: fully functional and billowing white voluminous clouds in all directions. The regulator hums a calming tone. Power flows steadily into the enlivened machinery. You carefully calibrate the regulator. It must be perfect. Too little power will cause a shutdown. Too much and the regulator will overcharge, potentially destroying everything that five generations of your family had worked so hard to create. You breathe a sigh of relief. All systems are nominal.

It was your mission to keep the station running for one year; to steer it to the ragged edge of a faraway coast, then return. You see dark water roiling below—deeper and more powerful than any body of water you have ever seen. You see a barrage of lightning. Then the station’s navigational tower splits and crashes to the ground like a dead tree felled by a multitool. The station rises above the storm, sputters, and goes silent. Finally, you see the station drifting aimlessly. You have no means of correcting course and no means of returning to the surface.

You are adrift. Alone.

You open your eyes. Sweat beads on your forehead even though the air is frigid. You have no idea how much time has passed. You wrap your cloak around yourself and look for the spirit, but find nothing.

You know now what you must do. The storm regulator is malfunctioning, causing the station to generate a seemingly endless snow storm. If you can disrupt the power supply, that should allow you to engage the emergency regulator, and use it to decrease the intensity of the storm. Or you can overcharge the current and disable the station, which you can only assume will cause the storm to cease entirely. Either way, you need to act quickly, for the storm continues to worsen with each passing moment.

Complete the GHOST OF A CHANCE mission. Discard Nimbus Spirit. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue. Search the General set for Ball Lightning and Cerberusian Cyclone and shuffle them into the path deck.

Gain the SILENCE THE STORM mission.

1.231

1.231a

You rush through the fierce winds to the malfunctioning regulator's control system. With each passing moment, the situation grows deadlier. Lightning crashes into the tower above you, sending glass shards and stone debris flying in all directions. The force of the wind nearly knocks you off your feet. You steady yourself in front of the control panel and take in the various mechanisms.

The consciousness of the station's keeper still flows through you. Understanding comes to you, unbidden. You begin systematically flicking switches and pressing buttons across the panel. It is as if operating the device is second nature to you. Then, still fighting the fierce winds, you pull the lever to the right of the panel. There is a hiss of steam and a whirring of machinery as several tubes disconnect from the central spire.

You watch as the current drains from the structure, until suddenly, the lights around the spire begin dimming with a wavering hum. For a moment, there is nothing but silence as all of the nearby machinery shuts off in sequence.

Then, another hum emerges, its pitch more stable and pleasing to the ear. Various secondary systems and emergency regulators kick in, and slowly, the storm changes before your very eyes. The harsh winds slow to a gentle crawl, and the dark clouds around the facility fade to a feathery gray. Instead of a raging blizzard, the snowfall feels serene.

You breathe a sigh of relief. Somehow, your plan worked. With the storm now under control, it should remain isolated over Sentinel Peak, and there is no longer the threat of the station crashing into the Earth. Perhaps now you can lay the spirit of the station's caretaker to rest.

Complete the SILENCE THE STORM and STORM BREWING missions. Record NIMBUS FIXED on the campaign tracker. Search the path deck, discard, and all play areas for Ball Lightning and Cerberusian Cyclone, and return them to the General set. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

Thanks to you, the storm around Nimbus Station is under control! You can safely return to the surface via the Observation Deck in the Undergrove.

1.231b

Between the raging storm, the howling wind, and the crackling electricity, the sound is deafening. You cover your ears and scramble to take refuge as the malfunctioning regulator shudders violently. The machines around you crack and shatter, sparks flying everywhere. Stone and glass debris rain down around you.

Peeking out from behind your safe haven, you watch as the the main spire of the primary regulator snaps in half. It rolls and crashes into the rest of the facility with a cacophonous sound, and a wave of dust rises from the impact, rushing outwards in all directions.

Then, silence. All of the lights around you flicker out. The instruments nearby cease their whirring, and all is still. When the dust finally settles, you realize that the clouds surrounding the station have become thin and wispy. The vents scattered around the facility are barely sputtering out cloud cover, and the snowfall has stopped, leaving only a few flakes drifting lazily on the now-gentle wind.

You breathe a sigh of relief, grateful that your desperate plan worked. The storm surrounding the facility is no more. The weather around the northern reaches of the Valley should return to normal in no time. Perhaps with the storm gone, you can lay the spirit of the station's caretaker to rest.

Complete the SILENCE THE STORM and STORM BREWING missions. Record NIMBUS SHUT DOWN on the campaign tracker. Search the path deck, discard, and all play areas for Ball Lightning and Cerberusian Cyclone, and return them to the General set. Return the Storm Regulator to the collection and permanently remove it from the Nimbus Facility set. Return the weather to the collection and replace it with the normal weather for today. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

1.232

Following the glimmering hoofprints leads you straight to your quarry. The elk is even more majestic than you could have imagined, composed entirely of radiant, glittering light. Throughout its body, the waning sunlight refracts into layered rainbows that cast the surrounding wilds in a lustrous glow, color peeling in all directions as if leaking out into the world. The vivid colors of the Valley seem almost dull in comparison to the elk's brilliance.

At your approach, the elk flattens its ears and takes a silent step forward, its eyes a sliver of prismatic light on a field of stars. Strange—it almost seems to recognize something in you.

Flip the TRUE COLORS mission over to its BATTLE OF WILLS side, but don't change your mission log. If you don't solve the puzzle of the mysterious elk today, you will have to track it down again.

Put Prismatic Elk into play along the way. Do not read its entry. Discard all shimmers in play to their appropriate discard piles. Shuffle all set-aside shimmers into their appropriate decks.

1.233

As you stare into the elk's eyes, a feeling of sudden confusion stirs within you. Then, a reflex: to flee, or to fight for your very life. You feel it swirling endlessly at the forefront of your mind. It's as if the elk is imparting its emotions to you—not something either one of you is currently feeling, but a spark of intense emotion nonetheless.

The truth is starting to reveal itself. This isn't an elk at all: it's an embodiment of this intense and sudden surge of emotion. A manifestation, perhaps? But where is the shaper who conjured it? How can it exist on its own like this?

Whatever it is, it's time for it to move on. Perhaps you can reason with these emotions, or simply overpower the manifestation with force.

Do not discard the Prismatic Elk. Keep each Ranger's  on it.

1.234

As the psychic apparition collapses, you pry its emotions from your mind. With one final strike, its form explodes in a radiant shower of glittering light. Color flashes in your eyes as its will fades. Thoughts and feelings rattle in your skull. You see claws, teeth. An atrox pounces. A final surge of power rattles through a broken conduit. Light erupts in all directions. It's over far, far too quickly.

With a final whimper of anguish, the remnants fade, leaving you with only a vague sense of loss. Color seeps back into the world. Within moments, the elk's light is gone for good.

Complete the TRUE COLORS (PRISMATIC ELK) mission. Permanently remove the Prismatic Elk from the Valley set. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue. Gain the Aspect of the Elk reward card.

Head back to Viv Terra at the Terra Lodge to tell her what happened.

1.235

You approach the elk carefully with peace, patience, and empathy. Opening your heart to the elk's emotions, a discussion of sorts ensues. Color swirls behind the pupils of your eyes. You see claws, teeth. An atrox pounces. A final surge of power rattles through a broken conduit. Light erupts in all directions. It can't be over. It can't.

You are a manifestation, you reply. Your shaper is long dead and has left this plane of existence. It's time to join him. It bucks against your words, but there is no disputing the truth. There is no bargain to be made with death, you tell it. Only understanding.

As it embraces your words, the emotions fade, leaving you with a warm, tender feeling. The elk's eyes dim and close as it fades from existence, dissolving into hundreds of pinpricks of glittering light that drift away on the gentle wind. Color seeps back into the world. Within moments, the elk's light is gone for good.

Complete the TRUE COLORS (PRISMATIC ELK) mission. Permanently remove the Prismatic Elk from the Valley set. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue. Gain the Aspect of the Elk reward card.

Head back to Viv Terra at the Terra Lodge to tell her what happened.

1.236

The moment you unveil the device the yeti gave you, a bizarre stillness falls over the region, and the Tenebrae's dissonant murmuring grows quiet. The device begins to stir in your hands, juddering rapidly and whirring as though trying to tell you something. Unsure what to do, you place it upon the ground and watch in amazement as it transforms before your eyes.

Its metal plating retracts to form a small dish, above which an orb of lustrous dark stone floats. It spins wildly in all directions like a broken compass. An invisible force ripples outward from the device, causing your skin to crawl and the hair on your arm to stand on end. Then, without warning, the plates extend once more, and the contraption shuts, clattering harmlessly to the ground.

You're not sure what precisely just happened, but it seems to have caused some change in the Tenebrae. You watch as they descend, moving as though tethered invisibly to the otherworldly device.

This must have been the yeti's purpose: to draw the Tenebrae somewhere. You recall the twisting sphere of supernatural light at the heart of the Otherwoods. Perhaps that is where it wishes you to take the Tenebrae.

Flip THE OTHER SIDE mission over to its CROSSING OVER side, but don't change your mission log. If you don't bring the Tenebrae to the Transdimensional Rift by the end of the day, you will have to track it down again.

1.237

You approach the unfathomable opening in space-time and once again set the yeti's device upon the ground. It whirs to life, deploying itself as though commanded, its central orb spinning madly.

Movement behind you catches your eye. You are relieved when it is only the yeti who approaches. Its gaze flits upward to the Tenebrae, which continue to descend and seem to shrink with each passing second.

You would ask the yeti what it intends to do if you thought for a moment there would be a response. Instead, you step back and watch as the strange contraption coaxes the Tenebrae closer and closer. The yeti approaches the rift, arm extended as though attempting to control it. It glances at you one last time with a grateful expression, then steps into the rift. You watch as its form is quickly tugged into the orb. The ghostly Tenebrae follow suit, whirling and roiling as they sink into its shape.

And then, just like that, they are gone. The device the yeti gave you remains behind, eerily idle. As you recover it, an unending series of questions plagues your mind. Why did the yeti want the Tenebrae? Where did they go? For that matter, where did they even come from?

You suppose you may never know the answers. For now, you take joy in the fact that the people of the Valley need no longer fear the specters that once haunted them. Perhaps you can find a use for the device the yeti left behind, as well.

Complete THE OTHER SIDE mission. Gain the Extraspatial Veil reward card. Return Entity from Afar and The Tenebrae to the collection. Permanently remove the following cards from their sets: Entity from Afar (from The Otherwoods set) and The Tenebrae (from the Valley set). Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

1.238

As you cross the stone bridge just outside the entrance to the Terra Lodge, you spot Viv Terra, keeper of the lodge, collecting logs from the nearby wood pile. You offer to help her carry wood inside, which she accepts with a soft smile. "I would appreciate it greatly. Please, make yourself at home. I'll brew up some elderberry tea."

You and Viv place the logs by the enormous stone fireplace, and you take a seat on one of the plush cushions arranged around the floor-level hearth. Viv re-enters a few minutes later carrying a steaming kettle and a tea set on an elegantly carved wooden tray. You accept a warm cup, taking small sips by the fire as you and Viv exchange pleasantries.

"I was growing worried about the storm to the north," she eventually admits, "but thank the ancestors, it seems to have quieted down." Then, spotting the expression on your face, she raises an eyebrow. "Might you know anything about that?"

You tell Viv to get ready for a tall tale, then explain to her everything you learned about the facility inside the stormcloud, and how you were able to prevent the storm from growing out of control.

"I knew there was something unnatural about that storm, but I never would have expected…" she trails off in disbelief. "And you are certain it's under control now?"

You nod—you're about as certain as you can be, given the circumstances.

"I have you to thank for the calm weather, then," she says, smile widening. "You may very well have saved our lodge from being buried by snow. In fact—wait here, please." She heads into a nearby storeroom and emerges moments later with a harness, a climbing hammer, and a set of metal pitons. "The climbing gear here once belonged to my forefathers, the founders of the Terra Lodge. They spent years scouring these mountains in search of the best location to construct this very lodge. I'd like you to have a set, as our way of saying thanks."

You nod and accept the climbing gear, thanking Viv for her hospitality.

"You are welcome here whenever you like," she says. "I hope this gear serves you well in your future travels."

Gain the Climbing Harness reward card.

If NIMBUS FIXED, this location has localized weather (Pristine Snow).

228.1

You approach the edge of the lake, its surface glinting in the sunlight. The spirit looks out over the crystalline waters with an unreadable expression. Slowly, his bearing starts to change. He seems lighter somehow; more at ease. Then the spirit begins to walk along the surface of the water before finally fading out of view.

Return HELPING HAND and Nimbus Spirit to the collection, then permanently remove Nimbus Spirit from the Arbor Mists set. Gain the Nimbus Spirit reward card.

If NIMBUS FIXED, this location has localized weather (Pristine Snow).

233.1

You see Viv in the parlor of the lodge, sitting on a cushion and quietly meditating. She is still as a rock, aside from the steady rise and fall of her chest as she breathes.

Clear Viv with  to sit and meditate with her.

233.2

"Hello again," Viv says as you approach the lodge. She ushers you inside, into the warmth of the lodge. The scent of freshly-cooked rabbit stew cooking on the hearth hits your nostrils, and you realize you're hungrier than you thought you were.

Viv wordlessly prepares you a bowl of stew. There's a change in her step, a lightness that wasn't present before. Finally, after you've taken a few heaping mouthfuls, she breaks the silence. "How are you faring? I hope confronting the elk was not too dangerous."

How did she know? Regardless, you might as well tell her what happened.

Clear Viv with  to tell her about the true nature of the elk.

233.3

You find Viv Terra just outside the lodge, clearing snow from the lodge's pitched roof with a rake. She glances down at you as you approach, and hops off of the ladder leaning against the side of the building. "How goes the hunt for the elk?"

Clear Viv with  to ask for help tracking the elk.

233.4

"Greetings," Viv Terra says as you enter the lodge's parlor. She is sitting cross-legged on one of the plush cushions in front of the fireplace, her eyes unwavering as they stare into the flickering flames. "I sense that the ancestors have something to tell us," she explains, as if in response to your unspoken question. "Please, sit with me awhile. Let's hear what they have to say."

Clear Viv with  to receive guidance from the ancestors.

233.5

Viv, still unmoving, peeks over at you with the slightest hint of a smile as you pull up a cushion and sit nearby. You close your eyes, empty your mind, and breathe, focusing your attention on your breath moving through your body.

An unmeasurable amount of time passes before Viv finally speaks. "It's good to see you. Care to talk awhile? I'll put some tea on."

Viv pours you a cup, and time washes away as you ponder the nature of the universe together.

Discard Viv. Each Ranger may either soothe 2 fatigue or add 3 to a spirit in play.

233.6

You must have a look about you, because the moment you sit with Viv, the lines of her face become etched with worry. "What's the matter?" she asks.

You tell Viv about your confrontation with the elk, and about its true nature. It was a psychic imprint left behind by a shaper who was caught unawares and mauled by an atrox. Their inability to accept their death resulted in a sudden surge of instinct and emotion, and one final manifestation: the elk.

"So that is the truth," Viv says, sighing. "I think I know of this shaper. He came to the lodge, once, maybe 15 years ago. He was headstrong and confident. And like so many shapers, thought himself invincible. He was headed north to see the great fortresses of the Ice Clans. We offered to accompany him to the Cascades beyond Sentinel Peak, but he would have none of it. We assumed he had left the Valley and never returned. How terrible for him." Viv is quiet for a moment. "Thank you for all you've done. You've brought peace to us all."

Record CLOSED THE CIRCLE on the campaign tracker. Discard Viv. Each Ranger may either soothe 2 fatigue or add 3 to a spirit in play.

233.7

"I wish I could be of more help," she says. "All I know for certain is that the elk tends to stay away from the Lodge. I'd look for it at the Crystal Basin if I were you––it will be far safer to confront it there than on the mountainside, if you're able to find it."

Discard Viv. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

233.8

Viv is silent for a long time as you sit in meditation together. Finally, she opens her eyes, peering at you as though in amazement. "Your arrival is no coincidence, that much is sure. The ancestors are urging me to share a tale. One which I have kept close to my heart for a very long time."

Curious, you nod and agree to hear out her story. She takes in a wavering breath. The words seem difficult for her to find. "It was many years ago, now. I was on a hunt with my husband. We found ourselves at the mercy of an atrox. We didn't see it until it was almost too late. But just then, an elk came charging at the atrox. Not just any elk—a being made out of shimmering rainbow light."

Viv pauses to gather herself, then continues. "My husband, he…he was closer to the atrox when it happened. The elk's antlers struck him. The atrox fled and the elk pursued, but the damage was already done. That was the day he died. I did not know it then, but I was pregnant with Oz at the time. Since his birth, I have seen the elk several times, but I have kept my distance."

You ask if it is a spirit, and she shakes her head. "Spirits act in accordance with their nature. But this elk does not. It is the bane of predators in these parts, seeking them out as though taking revenge for some past wrong."

Viv inches closer, clasping your hand in hers. "Please. Whatever this elk is, find it and bring peace to it. I forgave it for its part in my husband's death long ago, but according to the ancestors, there is more to this story. And I believe you are the ones to discover the truth. Will you help?"

Rangers Choose

A) Agree to track down the strange elk and figure out what it is.

Gain the TRUE COLORS (PRISMATIC ELK) mission. (Reminder: search the Valley set for Prismatic Elk and attach it facedown to the mission card.) Discard Viv.

B) If the ancestors will it, I will find time another day.

Discard Viv. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

233.9

You support Viv on your shoulder and carry her to the safety of the lodge. She sits by the fire in the parlor, quiet as a mouse, focusing on her breathing as you patch her up.

End the Day.

234.1

You find Oz hard at work in the small shed behind the lodge, his unstrung bow on his lap. He dips a stained rag into a wooden bowl of sweet smelling oil then massages it into the wood of his bow. "Hello! Need help with anything? Or…maybe you could take me out on a hunt?"

Clear Oz with  to take him hunting with you.

234.2

You find Viv's son Oz sitting by the lodge's rear window, watching the snow fall. "I know the snow makes things harder for us, but I really love it."

Suddenly, his eyes light up. "I have an idea! What if we made a person out of snow? I was thinking we would start with a really big snowball. Like this." Oz holds out his hands from each other as far as they will go. "Then we we'll make a slightly smaller one, and put it on top of it, and then ANOTHER one on top of THAT one. The last one's the head. What do you think?"

Clear Oz with  while the blizzard isn't raging to help him build a "snow person". The more snow has built up, the easier it will be.

234.3

Viv's son Oz stands by the clearing of effigies behind the lodge, whittling one of his own. He seems deep in thought—whatever he was trying to carve, he's lost the shape of it, whittling the dead branch down to its core.

You ask him what's on his mind, snapping him from his thoughts. "What? Oh. Nothing, really," he says. You sense that he might share his thoughts if given time. You sit nearby and look out at the glade. Oz sets the misshapen totem aside several others, then starts again with a fresh cut of wood.

Clear Oz with  to sit with Oz until he's ready to talk.

234.4

If there is a prey or predator in play, go to 234.5. Otherwise, continue reading:

Oz leads you into the woods not far from the lodge, and together you scour the surrounding wilderness for your quarry. Oz was taught well—he knows how to spot and identify various tracks, and is impressively silent as you trek through the woods. Unfortunately, you return to the lodge empty-handed, but you did get a good sense for what might be nearby.

Discard Oz. Scout 2 path cards. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

234.5

Oz leads you into the woods not far from the lodge, and together you follow the tracks of your quarry until you find it standing in a nearby clearing. You catch your breath. Oz stands stock-still. It hasn't noticed your approach. You quietly begin to discuss with Oz about what to do next, but he's already knocked his bow and taken aim. "Don't worry," he whispers. "I know what I'm doing."

The arrow is loosed, and in the space of a heartbeat, it strikes true.

Discard Oz. Add 4 to a prey or predator in play. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

234.6

Oz clips a pair of snowshoes to the bottom of his boots. With a hum, the soles separate into fragments held together by microgravitons—the very same memlev technology employed by artificers throughout the Valley since its invention more than 30 years ago. As Oz heads out into the snow, the shoes dynamically redistribute his weight, allowing him to walk effortlessly on the powdery white surface.

You trudge through the snow after Oz, following him to the clearing in front of the lodge. At Oz's instruction, you roll up a large ball of snow for the base, then a second smaller one for the torso, leaving the smallest one for the head. Then, Oz finds two curiously bent tree branches to serve as arms, while you attempt to assemble a face using whatever you have at hand. You look over your gear, and much to your surprise, inspiration strikes!

One at a time, for each snow on Pristine Snow, discard the top card from the challenge deck. At any point during this process, any Ranger may discard 1 or more gear (from their hand or play area) to help decorate the snow person. For each gear discarded in this way, discard additional cards from the top of the challenge deck equal to that gear's equip value.

Then, check to see if any of the discarded challenge cards have a  icon.

  • If none of them do, the resulting abomination is a droopy, half-melted mess. Oz gleefully knocks it over and soon begins again. Discard  from Oz until he has 1 fewer  than his threshold.
  • If at least 1 of them does, you succeed! Shuffle the challenge discard into the challenge deck, then go to 234.7.

234.7

You step back and admire your handiwork. "He's not very realistic, but I like him anyway," says Oz with a smile. The snowman won't last forever—he's liable to melt once the sun returns—but for now, he stands smiling by the side of the lodge, waving hello.

"Thanks for the help!" Oz says as you make your way back to the lodge. You mention how much you like his snowshoes, and Oz beams with pride. "Thanks! Elder Orlin donated a whole bunch of them to the lodge back when my grandfather was still alive. Do you want a pair? We have a whole crate full of them. I'm sure my mom would approve, if you're worried about that." He runs off and returns with another pair of snowshoes, handing them to you. "They're good for more than just snow. I hope you like them!"

Record BUILT A SNOWMAN on your campaign tracker. Discard Oz and gain the Memlev Snowshoes reward card.

234.8

"Right now?" Oz glances out the window. "It's too windy out there. I may love the snow, but I'm not gonna go out there until the blizzard dies down."

Discard  from Oz until he has 1 fewer  than his threshold.

234.9

Soon enough, Oz opens up to you. "It's just…this place…it means a lot to me and my mom. My great grandpa and grandma built the main hall of the lodge way back when we first came up through the Bitter Pass. These effigies, they're not just to honor the beings we've hunted. They're a record of nearly every hunter that's ever visited the lodge, plus my whole family!. It's been around for more than a hundred years. And when Mom passes…" He trails off.

You think you understand what's troubling him now. When Viv passes, it'll likely be up to Oz to maintain the lodge and keep it running. It must be a lot of responsibility on the shoulders of one so young. You tell Oz that his future is not set in stone. His mother is still young, besides—there is plenty of time before that responsibility falls to him.

He nods absently. You sit in comfortable silence awhile longer. Oz continues to carve the effigy, and it steadily takes shape.

Discard Oz. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

234.10

Oz cries out, doubling over with pain. "Don't tell my mom?" he pleads. "I promised her I'd be safe."

You tell him that there's no way around that, lifting him in your arms and carrying him home.

End the Day.

235.1

You begin the arduous task of carving your own effigy—first finding a suitable cut of wood in the shed behind the lodge, then using a compact carbon-forged knife to carve the effigy's unique features. You recall them as clear as day…

Choose a being from the following list that was the subject of a mission you have completed. Permanently move that being to the Terra Lodge set and attach it faceup to the Terra Lodge location, as an effigy.

  • Quiet
  • Umbra
  • Ol' Bloody Clicker
  • Wild Scuttlebus
  • Deadfall
  • Prismatic Elk
  • Strix
  • Rampha
  • Sylvas

236.1

The rift roils with energy and thrums with uproarious sound. You consider traveling through the rift back to the Otherwoods, but first you'll need to decide if you can stomach another trip through space.

Venture inside the rift to travel back to the Otherwoods. The more  icons attached to the rift, the more likely you are to succeed.

236.2

Once again, you come to the source of the disturbances in these strange woods: the tear in reality, still buzzing with discordant noise and swirling with light and color. After what you've seen during your brief stint inside the rift, it seems like you can cross great distances in an instant. Perhaps you can use the rift to travel elsewhere, if you feel up to it.

Venture inside the rift to travel to another destination. The more  icons attached to the rift, the more likely you are to succeed.

236.3

The deeper you venture beyond the eaves of the Otherwoods, the more you begin to think you've left the Valley behind. The leaves are the wrong shade of green, and there is far too much blue and red in the underbrush. You hear distant birds calling to one another with unfamiliar voices. Finally, you come to a small clearing where the trees arch and twist like curved claws, closing in around you. At its center is what you can only describe as a tear in reality; a curtain of sorts, surrounded by waves of distorted air and shimmering energy. Within its shape is a twisting corridor of kaleidoscopic light and cacophonous sound.

Venture inside the rift to learn more. The more  icons attached to the rift, the more likely the attempt will be successful. The more  icons attached to the rift, the more dangerous the attempt will be.

236.4

IF YOU ARE NOT AT THE OTHERWOODS, GO TO 236.5

IF YOU JOURNEYED BEYOND, GO TO 236.6

OTHERWISE, READ:

With trepidation, you approach the rift, wondering what—if anything—could be on the other side. Curiosity gnaws at you like a hunger. There's only one way to find out. Your heart thudding in your chest, you venture inside, emerging somewhere else entirely…

Look at 2 of the challenge cards attached to Transdimensional Rift, at random.

READ THE ENTRY BASED ON THE CHALLENGE ICONS ON THE LOOKED-AT CARDS:

 :

Somehow, the memories of your journey begin to slip away the second you re-emerge. You'd ventured into another realm, wondrous and alien in ways you could scarcely imagine, and yet…it's all muddled and fuzzy in your head. Just an assortment of jumbled images and sensations. Was it all just a vision? Or was it real? Perhaps you'll never know. But now that you know the rift can cross great distances across space, you might be able to use it to your advantage.

Record JOURNEYED BEYOND on your campaign tracker. Soothe 2 fatigue. You may discard the 2 looked-at cards (or keep them attached to Transdimensional Rift).

 :

You find yourself in a sensationless void, an emptiness that stretches infinitely before you. Your mind begs for solid ground to walk on, for fresh air to breathe, for something you can touch and call real. Then, as if nothing had truly happened, the rift spits you back out into the Otherwoods, no worse for wear. Your addled brain has no sense of how much time has passed.

Nothing happens. You may discard the 2 looked-at cards (or keep them attached to Transdimensional Rift).

 :

You walk forward into the rift and are immediately sent flying backward. Your back slams against a nearby tree, and you collapse to the ground. Pain sears through your spine.

Suffer one injury. You may discard the 2 looked-at cards (or keep them attached to Transdimensional Rift).

 :

You see the Otherwoods as they once were; a lush, beautiful forest of autumnal yellows and reds. Your vision swims as the Valley stretches before you, the fractured wall intact. A vast body of water lies beyond it, glimmering in the sun. Your awareness is drawn upward, and for a brief moment you glimpse the majesty of the Earth. Countless stars twinkle throughout the cosmos beyond.

Then you feel your consciousness yanked back to the Earth, and are surprised to find yourself in the Otherwoods once more. Somehow, you've traveled an immeasurable distance in mere moments. Perhaps you can use this rift to your advantage, if you can stomach another journey.

Record JOURNEYED BEYOND on your campaign tracker. Suffer 2 fatigue. You may discard the 2 looked-at cards (or keep them attached to Transdimensional Rift).

 :

You emerge shaking and sweating. Your heart pounds in your chest. But despite your terror, you cannot bring yourself to remember what exactly it is you saw—only glimpses. Charred ground. Rolling hills of blackened trees. A sky choked with ash. There was no sound. No life. Only the aftermath of something horrible.

Though you have no desire to recreate the experience, it's clear that the rift can cross great distances across space. Perhaps you can use it to your advantage—if you can avoid venturing to such a realm again.

Record JOURNEYED BEYOND on your campaign tracker. Suffer 1 injury. You may discard the 2 looked-at cards (or keep them attached to Transdimensional Rift).

 :

Your vision swims with brilliant light and vivid color. You can scarcely comprehend what you are seeing. The light rushes past you, a cascading corridor of ever-shifting holographic curves and shapes. There is a deafening sound like the rushing of wind as the tunnel expands. There is no end in sight. With some resistance, you finally manage to pry yourself out, collapsing onto your back in exhaustion.

Suffer 2 fatigue. You may discard the 2 looked-at cards (or keep them attached to Transdimensional Rift).

236.5

At your approach, the exit to the rift expands into a tunnel of kaleidoscopic colors. Dissonant whispering coaxes you onward as you step forth into the ingress. It takes only a few strides for you to cross the corridor. If you're lucky, it will be the Otherwoods that greets you on the other end…

Look at 2 of the challenge cards attached to Transdimensional Rift, at random.

  • If none have a  icon, the attempt fails. You may discard any of the looked-at cards.
  • If at least one has a  icon, you may immediately proceed to the travel phase and travel to The Otherwoods (even if there are ready cards with the Obstacle keyword in play). Build the path deck using Old Growth.

236.6

Your destination set firmly in your mind, you boldly step into the rift. Swirling color and pandemonious noise greet you. A familiar corridor of dazzling light expands before you, and in the distance, you catch only brief glimpses of the place you wish to go. All around you, the Valley's rolling hills and mountains fly, their peaks warped and twisted. You walk forward with your eyes closed, focusing on your destination. As soon as you feel the fresh breeze on your skin, you open your eyes to reveal where the rift has taken you…

Look at 2 of the challenge cards attached to Transdimensional Rift, at random.

  • If none have a  icon, you find yourself still in the Otherwoods, no worse for wear. You may discard any of the looked-at cards.
  • If at least one has a  icon, the rift has taken you precisely where you wished to go. You may immediately proceed to the travel phase and travel to any location that you have already been to at least once (even if there are ready cards with the Obstacle keyword in play). Build the path deck using any terrain set that could lead to the chosen location. Shuffle the Transdimensional Rift into this new path deck.

237.1

Behind you, flanked by trees, you can make out the dark outline of a familiar, furry shape. You wonder if perhaps this time the yeti is the one following you, and not the other way around. Are you the first human it's ever interacted with? You wonder if there is anything it can do to help you, given that it seems to understand these woods better than you ever could.

Clear the Entity from Afar with  to ask for its help with the Transdimensional Rift.

237.2

Movement flutters in your periphery. It's barely a silhouette, broken by rays of light. As quickly as it appeared, it vanishes, gone before your very eyes.

You've seen a being like this before, in the woods near the Northern Outpost. A yeti! How incredible! You wonder if this is the same yeti you encountered before, or one of its kin. Either way, you know that you may never have another opportunity to find out.

Clear the Entity from Afar with  to attempt to track down the yeti.

237.3

Movement flutters in your periphery. It's barely a silhouette, broken by rays of light. Was that a person? You rush to where you thought you saw it, but find nothing. Where could it have gone? And more importantly…what was it?

Clear the Entity from Afar with  to attempt to track down the mysterious figure.

237.4

You show the yeti the device it gave you, hoping to learn more about its function. It approaches and twists the contraption around, unraveling the metallic plates to expose a spinning orb of dark stone. It hovers its hand over the orb for a moment and hums a dissonant note. Then it shuts the device closed once more, and disappears into thin air, as is its wont. You can only hope that whatever the yeti did, it helped, somehow.

If Transdimensional Rift is in play, scout the top 2 cards of the challenge deck. You may attach either of them facedown to Transdimensional Rift. Discard all  from Entity from Afar.

If Transdimensional Rift is not in play, scout the top 2 cards of the Otherworld deck. Discard all  from Entity from Afar.

237.5

You chase the yeti down as best you can, weaving between trees and sloshing through mud. Every time you come close, it vanishes once more, just beyond your grasp. Eventually, you come to a ridge overlooking the Otherwoods, where the yeti stands like a statue. It almost seems to beckon you closer.

You approach warily, not wanting it to run off again, but this time it makes no effort to flee or hide. It merely raises a long-fingered hand and gestures toward the south.

You peer into the distance. There, far on the horizon, past the Otherwoods, you spot the ghostly forms of the Tenebrae. They drift through the sky like sinister clouds. A shudder worms through your spine.

The yeti slowly reaches for your hand, turns your palm upward, and there places a strange device—a spherical object composed of interlocking metallic plates. Then it touches your forehead with its index finger, and a flash of light overtakes your vision.

Your consciousness is pulled as though by a chain. You catch a brief glimpse of something deep in the Otherwoods, a clearing where the trees curve outward as though by force. In its center, you see what you can only imagine to be the source: a multicolored veil of pulsing light that warps and blurs the longer you look at it.

Then, suddenly, you are back in your body. The yeti stares at you intently. You're not completely sure what it wants you to do, but it must have something to do with the Tenebrae and the strange phenomenon in that clearing.

RANGERS CHOOSE:

A) Agree to do whatever you think the yeti is asking you to do.

You nod and accept the device, hoping you understand the yeti's intent. Though it speaks not a word, you sense something akin to friendship growing between you. Then, before you can speak, it unceremoniously flickers out of existence as though folding out of three-dimensional space.

Gain THE OTHER SIDE mission. Discard Entity from Afar.

B) Sorry, this is beyond me.

You hand the device back to the yeti and shake your head. You have no idea what it wants you to do, and no particular desire to venture near the Tenebrae. The yeti gracefully takes the device and vanishes behind a nearby tree.

Discard Entity from Afar. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

237.6

You try to chase the entity down, but it vanishes before you can catch up to it. The last you saw, it looked like it was headed south, in the direction of the Northern Outpost.

Discard Entity from Afar. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

Travel to the Northern Outpost to find the mysterious entity.

240.1

You hold on tight to the mag-grappler on the spiderline you created, and prepare to leap from the edge of the deck.

You may immediately proceed to the travel phase and travel to Sentinel Peak (even if there are ready cards with the Obstacle keyword in play). Build the path deck using the Mountain Pass terrain set. Search the General set for Snow-Capped Summit and put it into play.

241.1

As you approach the center of the crystal-blue stream that flows through the grove, you spot the spirit you've been searching for. He sits by the babbling water, deep in meditation. Above him, pipes send a cascade of purified water into the stream. From this small waterfall, the water flows in two directions, deeper into the mist.

From what you've learned, this must be the spirit of the keeper who was responsibile for maintaining the facility. He seems unaware of your presence, the sound of falling water and the gentle murmuring of the stream creating the perfect environment for deep meditation. Thinking back on long discussions with Nal during your training, you recall that speaking with spirits is not entirely different from speaking with beings of flesh and blood. You need only remain compassionate and make your intentions clear.

Move your  to the spirit to commune with it and learn more about the facility.

241.2

Once again, you notice the spirit of the Nimbus Station's keeper as he roams through the mist, following the stream that cuts through the woods. For once, he seems to acknowledge your presence, glancing in your direction and waiting as if beckoning you to follow. With the weather around the station finally under control, you wonder what it is the spirit wishes to show you.

Clear the spirit with  to follow it.

241.3

Through the mist, you spot a figure weaving between the trees. Is there somebody else up here? How did he get here?

You follow the figure along the crystalline stream that flows through the woods, finally getting close enough to call out to him. He glances toward you, but there is no response. Perhaps it's just the mist, but he seems almost ethereal, like a figment of your imagination. As you approach, you realize the truth: it's not a living man, but the spirit of one. Somebody who used to live here, perhaps?

Clear the spirit with  to observe it.

241.4

The forlorn spirit stares your way in perfect silence. You can still see the longing in his eyes. You ponder your route, wondering if you can still afford to take the spirit to the Crystal Basin, as promised.

RANGERS CHOOSE:

A) Continue escorting the spirit. Discard all  from Nimbus Spirit.

B) Part ways. Discard Nimbus Spirit and return HELPING HAND to the collection. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

241.5

You follow the spirit through the mist until finally, he raises a hand as if instructing you to halt. It doesn't take long before you realize why: you've reached the very edge of the woods, where the ground opens up to the clouds below. The stream you've been walking alongside spills over the side of the floating island, dissipating as mist into the cold air.

The spirit walks to the edge and peers over the side longingly. You can only imagine the spirit's loneliness after centuries spent up here in quiet solitude. He must have had no way of setting foot back on the surface. Perhaps you can accompany him to the surface, where he can finally rest.

RANGERS CHOOSE:

A) Follow me, I'll take you to the Crystal Basin. Discard all  and attach the HELPING HAND mission to Nimbus Spirit.

B) Sorry, I'm not headed in that direction. Discard Nimbus Spirit. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

241.6

Try as you might to communicate with the spirit, he doesn't react to your presence. You imagine he has grown used to being alone up here. You watch as he tends to some of the machinery found in the grove, their esoteric controls hidden amongst the trees. Then, with a brief glance in your direction, the spirit fades into the deep fog that permeates the woods.

Discard Nimbus Spirit. Either discard a Deep Fog from play, or each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

243.1

You spot Remi meandering through the woods, a strut in his step and a whistle on his lips. He looks quite different without his enormous backpack looming over his shoulders. "Ah, it's you!" he says, hustling over to clasp your hand. "Looking for the treehouse? You're more than welcome to it anytime you like."

Clear Remi with  to have him lead you to his treehouse.

243.2

Remi is busy planing wood for his treehouse using a laser cutter when you find him. Sweat drips from his brow. A few other people are working nearby with tools of their own, pitching in to create Remi's dream home. Pulleys carry planks of wood and wall frames up to the treetops, where several more people wait to put them in place.

It's clear now what Remi was carrying in his overstuffed backpack—an array of advanced construction tools that make it possible to build such a complex structure in the trees.

It looks like he's already got help, but that doesn't mean he won't accept more from an able-bodied Ranger such as yourself.

Clear Remi with  to pitch in.

243.3

You see Remi resting by the side of the trail, his enormous backpack leaned up against a nearby boulder. "Ah!" he says as you approach. "Fancy meeting you here. Have time to keep an old man company for a while?"

Clear Remi with  to see how his search has gone.

243.4

Just ahead, you spot a man dwarfed by his enormous backpack. He looks aged and well-traveled, and wears a jovial grin. "Why hello there!" he calls out to you as you approach.

You ask what he's doing out here in the wilds with what appears to be his entire life on his shoulders. "Oh, this?" He hefts his backpack with a grin. "Ah, y'see, I tend to stay on the trail. Don't have much of a place where I can settle down, at least, not yet, anyway…"

Clear Remi with  to ask what he means.

243.5

Remi leads you through the woods. Soon, the spiraling wooden stairway leading to Remi's treehouse comes into view. Above, you spot several wooden bridges that creak as they sway gently in the breeze. The main structure of the treehouse looks no worse for wear. You suspect he loves his home more than anyone you've ever met.

"Well, I'm off!" Remi says, patting your back. "Make yourself at home!"

Search the path deck and discard for Remi's Treehouse and put it into play. Do not read its entry. Discard Remi Byrd. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

243.6

You take some of the logs that Remi has gathered, roll up your sleeves, and get to work planing the wood, creating boards for the floor of the treehouse. Time whittles away as you focus on the task, and everything else fades from your mind.

When you're done, you coordinate with the other helpers to get the freshly-cut planks of wood up to the treetops, then take a short rest while Remi heads off to find more materials for the treehouse.

Discard Remi Byrd. Cross off 246.3 on the day track and record it one day earlier. If that day is today, immediately proceed to 243.7. (Do not read 246.3.)

243.7

The work is long and taxing, but eventually, you and Remi finish the main structure. It is built around the wide trunk of a solitary giant, with a wraparound wooden balcony that peers down at the forest floor below.

Remi examines his new home with pride. "It's perfect," he says, his eyes welling with tears. "Everything I dreamed of and more. Don't worry about the rest, I can take it from here." He shakes your hand vigorously. "I'm forever in your debt. My home is always open."

Record REMI'S TREEHOUSE BUILT on your campaign tracker. Permanently move Remi's Treehouse from the General set to the set corresponding to this location. (If that location is not pivotal, bundle Remi's Treehouse next to that location, as a reminder.) Then, put Remi's Treehouse into play. Do not read its entry.

243.8

Remi takes the lead as you explore the area together, on the lookout for a copse of trees that will serve for his treehouse. Perhaps he'll make this spot his new home.

If this location is not a forest, or if it is crossed off from the list in entry 243.12, discard all  from Remi Byrd. Otherwise, continue reading:

DISCARD THE TOP CARD OF THE CHALLENGE DECK AND READ THE ENTRY THAT CORRESPONDS TO ITS CHALLENGE SYMBOL:

:

Remi's eyes brighten as you show him around, his smile infectious. "It's perfect," he says at last. "I can build the main structure up there. It's just wide enough to support the stairway leading up to the treehouse. And see that cluster of trees over there? Wait, let me show you…"

He rummages through his backpack for a small sketchbook and starts showing you some drawings he made of his dream home. Remi was right—this spot will work perfectly for what he's envisioned.

"Thank you for showing me this spot. I just need some building materials and I'll be ready to start construction. It should only take me a few days or so. Why don't you drop by once I'm done? I'm sure it'll beat sleeping on the cold, hard earth!"

Return HELPING HAND to the collection. Set Remi Byrd aside. When you travel away from this location, permanently move Remi Byrd to the set corresponding to this location. (If this location is not pivotal, bundle Remi Byrd next to this location, as a reminder.) Record TREEHOUSE UNDER CONSTRUCTION on the campaign tracker and 246.3 five days from now on the day track.

:

Remi hems and haws. "I just don't know…" he says, stroking his beard. "I like these woods well enough, but we haven't found the right cluster of trees to form the foundation." Perhaps you should show him around a little more so he gets a better sense of the space.

Discard all  from Remi Byrd.

Clear Remi with  again to show him around some more.

:

Remi thinks for a while, then shakes his head. "I'm sorry. It's a beautiful forest, but it just doesn't feel like home to me. Besides, I'm afraid none of these trees will work well for my dream home. The main structure needs one with enough space around it for a wraparound deck, but just enough trees clustered nearby to support the other structures." He's right, unfortunately—this area won't work for what he's envisioned. Maybe there's another forest nearby that will work.

Discard all  from Remi Byrd. Cross off this location from the list on 243.12. You'll have to take him somewhere else to see if he likes it there. If all locations on the list are crossed off, go to 243.13.

243.9

You gesture to Remi's backpack and guess aloud that he hasn't had any luck finding the right forest for his new home. He chuckles and nods. "You have the right of it, my friend. No luck just yet. I appreciate your help, though. I don't suppose you have time to accompany me to another forest, do you? I'm sure there's a spot nearby that will work for what I'm envisioning."

RANGERS CHOOSE:

A) I'm sure you're right! Let's get to it. Discard all  and attach the HELPING HAND mission to Remi Byrd.

Bring Remi to a forest location and clear him with  again to see if he wants to build his treehouse there. See 243.12 for a list of potential sites.

B) Sorry Remi, perhaps some other time. Discard Remi. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

243.10

You ask Remi why he has nowhere to settle down. "I want to live out in the wilds, among the trees," he explains with a yearning expression. His eyes almost seem to drift into dream as he describes his ideal home—a simple, solitary treehouse in the middle of the forest, away from the hustle and bustle of the villages, where he can live out the rest of his days in peace and quiet. "And I'd like to build it without a shaper twisting the wood around like it's nothing. Want to use my own two hands to build it. Nothing quite like the rush of a job well done, don't you think? All I have to do is find the right spot, that's all. Somewhere high in the treetops."

It's an admirable goal, but doing that all on his own sounds daunting. Even just finding the right spot in the right forest could take a lifetime. Maybe there's something you can do to help.

RANGERS CHOOSE:

A) Offer to help Remi find a spot for his treehouse.

"Really? You'd do that?" Remi's grin somehow grows even wider. "I'll tell you what. You find me a perfect place for my treehouse, and I'll let you stay there whenever you're in the area. Deal?"

Bring Remi to a forest location and clear him with  again to see if he wants to build his treehouse there. See 243.12 for a list of potential sites.

B) Wish Remi luck and bid him farewell. Discard Remi. Each Ranger soothes 2 fatigue.

243.11

Remi clutches his chest and collapses. You rush to his side and examine his injury. "I've got first aid supplies in my pack," he says weakly, motioning behind him. You tell him there's no need and take out your own first aid kit, then set to work bandaging his wounds. He'll need substantial time to rest and recover.

End the Day.

243.12

Use this list of forest locations to keep track of which locations you have already brought Remi to. (You may wish to write this list down on your campaign tracker or on a separate piece of paper.)

  • Lone Tree Station
  • Ancestor's Grove
  • The Philosopher's Garden
  • The High Basin
  • Branch
  • Biological Outpost
  • The Cypress Citadel
  • The Otherwoods
  • Terra Lodge
  • Arbor Mists

243.13

"Well, that settles it," Remi says. "If there's no forest in the Valley that will suit my dream home, I'll just have to look elsewhere."

You ask Remi where he'll search next, and he considers the question carefully. "The Verdessa would be the logical place, I suppose." Your eyes widen. The Verdessa is well known for its many dangers, and is not considered habitable by most Valley dwellers. He places a calloused hand on your shoulder and grins. "Don't worry, I'll be fine. Thank you so much for all your help. I truly appreciate it."

With that, Remi adjusts his enormous backpack and sets off southward. You can only hope that someday, he finds it.

Record REMI WENT SOUTH on the campaign tracker. Return Remi to the collection and permanently remove him from the Valley set.

244.1

Taking hold of the mag-grappler on the spiderline you've created, you take a deep breath and leap from the summit, riding the line into the clouds around the floating island. You can only hope the nanowire—and your grip—hold out through the heavy winds.

You may immediately proceed to the travel phase and travel to The Undergrove (even if there are ready cards with the Obstacle keyword in play). Build the path deck using the Nimbus terrain set.

245.1

You enter the treehouse and find it well appointed and stocked. It's a welcome reprieve from sleeping out in the wilds. You have no doubt you'll get a full night's rest in here, safe from any threats down in the forest floor.

Record 245.2 tomorrow on the day track.

End the day. You are considered to have camped.

245.2

You awaken feeling more refreshed than you have in days. Remi seems to have left out a hearty breakfast for you along with a note: "Thanks for stopping by! You're welcome here anytime." With your stomach full and your body caught up on sleep, you bundle up your belongings and get ready for the day ahead.

Search the path deck for Remi's Treehouse and put it into play. Do not read its entry. Each Ranger moves their  to Remi's Treehouse.

Each Ranger shuffles their starting hand back into their deck, then searches their deck for any six cards to be their starting hand.

246.1

As the light of dawn breaks, your gaze is guided toward the northern horizon by the flight of a bird, and there your eyes settle on something unusual: a dark, menacing stormcloud whirling in place over Sentinel Peak, the white-capped mountain at the very northern end of the Valley. You recognize it as a cloud laden with snow—quite unusual weather for this time of year. A chill breeze blows from that direction, and a sense of foreboding fills your heart. There is something strange about that cloud. You resolve to investigate as soon as you are able.

Gain the STORM BREWING mission.

Head north to investigate the strange storm.

246.2

READ ONE:

If you are at The Undergrove, Arbor Mists, or Nimbus Facility:

You wake to a massive quake and the world tilting. Gathering your things, you sprint to the edge of the island and watch as it descends, aiming directly for Sentinel Peak. It seems the machinery keeping the island afloat has finally given out, and it's poised to crash into the mountain.

You can't be caught up here when the island crashes. You have only one choice: wait until it's descended low enough to take a leap of faith, aiming for the lake south of Sentinel Peak. You rush down stairs and over catwalks as you try to get lower. Then you take in a deep breath and jump over the side of the railing.

The icy depths swallow you whole. You swim to the surface and watch as the island crashes into the mountainside. A massive avalanche follows in the wake of the crash, burying the outlying region in snow, dust, and debris. When the rumbling ceases, there is no more storm hanging over the northern end of the Valley…and no more facility, either.

Complete all Nimbus missions. Swap your starting location for Crystal Basin. Build the path deck using the Lakeshore terrain set.

Each Ranger shuffles a Lingering Injury malady into their deck.

The stormcloud—and the island within—have crashed to the ground. Record NIMBUS LOST on the campaign tracker. You can no longer travel to The Undergrove, Arbor Mists, or Nimbus Facility.

Otherwise:

You wake to a rolling, thunderous crash and immediately find a hilltop from which you can view the source of the devastation. To the north, the ominous stormcloud that has made its home beside Sentinel Peak has finally moved. It seems to have drifted into the mountainside, and something solid within has crashed into its icy slopes.

A massive avalanche follows in the wake of the crash, burying the outlying region in snow. A cloud of dust and debris rises from the north as the structure inside the stormcloud finally settles. By the time the rumbling ceases, there is no more storm hanging over the northern end of the Valley. You'll never know the truth behind the mystery of the strange storm, and the mountain in its shadow will forever be changed by the catastrophic crash.

Complete all Nimbus missions.

The stormcloud—and the island within—have crashed to the ground. Record NIMBUS LOST on the campaign tracker. You can no longer travel to The Undergrove, Arbor Mists, or Nimbus Facility.

1.101

If you’re RUNNING LATE, go to entry 1.101a. Otherwise, if you don't have the SEARCH BELOW (SILARO MAKO) mission, read the following:

At the base of a massive cylinder of forged carbon, you see several lambance rigs illuminating the space in a wide ring. Within, there are the beginnings of a base camp. The advance team moves about the space, taking stock, unpacking crates, setting up equipment, and building temporary structures. Beyond, there is a vast darkness, sparkling with a strange kind of precipitation that illuminates the boundaries of the cavern as it falls. Shadows move and shift as the droplets pass. The effect is mesmerizing.
